what_it_does: A 390px native-mobile HTML mockup of the A2 canvas view showing a running chain (codebase content node → ask process node) with a full-bleed canvas, bottom-sheet inspector, bottom tab-bar, thumb run button, semantic-zoom indicator, and activity strip.
observations: Forces a 390px phone frame via scoped CSS while relying on design-system.css for component language
Uses system classes and variables (.node, .mono-chip, .sdot, .btn.run, .tabbar, --kind-*, etc.)
Represents only two live node kinds: process and content; model is shown as a config pill, not a kind
Status dots are limited to idle/ran/cached/stuck; 'await' is described as an Inbox badge, never a node dot
Includes a peeked bottom-sheet inspector for the selected 'ask' node
Shows semantic zoom via a 0.8× expanded indicator and a collapsed neighbour chip
Has a bottom-right thumb 'run' FAB and a four-item tab-bar (Canvas · Inbox · RHM · Run)
Contains extensive HTML comments documenting representational constraints and code references
No code symbols are present; symbols[] is empty
standouts: Explicit 'no fiction' contract in comments — it refuses to show presentation nodes, 'await'/'failed' dots, a 4-kind legend, or Vector ports that are not in the real chain
Native-mobile form is intentionally not a shrunk desktop: full-bleed canvas, bottom sheet, thumb-reach FAB
Semantic-zoom state is made visible through both an indicator and a collapsed chip
The 'volatile→ran' pill and activity strip surface the memo-gate / ENG-resolve behaviour
conventions: Follows the shared design-system.css vocabulary for nodes, chips, status dots, buttons, and tab-bar
Uses data-ui-ref attributes to tag UI surfaces for cross-referencing
Scoped local CSS handles only the 390px layout chrome; node styling stays system-level
HTML comments act as a design spec / inventory mapping
concerns: Heavy inline documentation could drift from the actual implementation it references
Hardcoded 390px width and magic-number SVG edge coordinates make the layout brittle
Scoped CSS duplicates mobile-chrome logic that design-system.css already claims to handle below 699px
Bottom sheet is static HTML with no visible drag/expand interaction or accessibility semantics
notes: A phone-native A2 canvas mockup that tries hard to keep the UI vocabulary honest to the underlying node/status model.
questions: Is the bottom sheet actually draggable/expandable, or is it only a visual peeked state?
How would the 'await' Inbox badge render and update in the tab-bar?
Does the semantic-zoom threshold of 0.5 and pinch gesture exist in code, or is it only illustrated?
How do the referenced code files (registry.py, suite.py, scheduler.py, App.tsx) relate to this mockup beyond documentation?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it is a native-mobile canvas mockup that depicts the running chain using only the real kinds, statuses, and ports described in the comments.
apparent_themes: Mobile form changes, flow doesn't (J7 UX law)
Truth-to-code mockups: UI must not invent states or kinds not present in the system
Semantic zoom and information density on small screens
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to define a faithful, phone-native canvas surface that surfaces the same chain semantics as the desktop A2 view without adding misleading UI elements.
novelty: The mockup carries an explicit representational contract in its comments, pairing a native-mobile layout with a strict 'no fiction' rule about which node kinds, statuses, and ports may be shown.
contribution: A concrete mobile canvas layout and a documented set of representational constraints (two node kinds, four status dots, model-as-config, await-as-badge) that a fused design system could reuse for native mobile canvas chrome.
summary_for_embedding: A 390px native-mobile HTML mockup of the A2 canvas showing a running codebase→ask chain with a bottom-sheet inspector, tab-bar, thumb run button, semantic-zoom indicator, and strict representational constraints.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a phone-native canvas artifact that accurately surfaces the running chain's real node kinds, statuses, and memo-gate behaviour without inventing UI elements.
